folio.label: 124r
folio.l: Nat but witú fyue or sexe , of hire meyne
And in a trenche , fortú in the Park gotú she
¢ The vapor , whicú , that from the erthe glode
Made the Sonne to seme , rody and brode
But nathelees it was , so fair a sigút
That it made alle here hertes , for to ligút
What for the Sesou~ , & the morwenyngö
What for the foules , that she herd syngö
For rigút a non , she wist what they ment
Rigút by here songö , & knew al here entent
¢ The knotte why , that euäy tale is told
If it be taried , til the lust be cold
Of hem , that after it , han herkened ©ore
The sauour passetú , euäe lengere the more
For fulsumnesse , of his prolixitee
And by this same resou~ , thynketú me
I shulde vn to the knot , condescende
And maken of here walkyngö , sone an ende
¢ A mydde a tree ful drye , as white as chalk
As Canacee was pleiyngö , in hire walk
There sat a faucou~ , ouer hire hed ful heye
That witú a pitous voice , so gan to crye
That al the wode , resouned of hire cry
I betyn had she hire self , so pitously
With bothe hire wenges , til the red blod
Ran endelongö the tree , there as she stood
And euäe in on alwey , she cried and shrigút
And witú hire beek , hire seluen she so twigút
That there nas tygre , ne so cruel beste
That dwelletú either in wode , or in foreste
That nolde han wept , if that he wepe coude
For sorwe of hire , she shrigút alwey so loude
¢ For æäe was neuäe ©et , noman a lyue
If that I coude , a faucon wel discryue
That herd of swicú an other , of fairnesse
As wel of plumage , as of gentilnesse
Of shap~ , of al that mygút I rekened be
A faucon ¹egryn , than semed she
Of fremd land , and euäemo there she stod
She swougúned now and now , for lakke of blood
Til wol ny is she fallen , fro the Tree
This faire kynges dougúter , Canacee
That on hire fyngre , bar the queynt Ryngö
Thurgú whicú , she vnderstod wel , euäy thyngö
That any foul , may in his ledne seyn
